metadata.notes: Conservative advisory limits. Linear approximation per breakpoint band. Matches Excel formula exactly including IFNA fallback. maxCap=37 KT applied per FCOM Tab 2.29.2a (Excel formula G8: =IF(G7>37,37,G7)).

RWYCC 5 (Good) advisory limits. Same bracketedLinear strategy as Dry, with 6 brackets (additional crosswindKnots=15 endpoint per Excel 'Good 788' sheet). Uniform slope 0.06, uniform intercept step +4. Shares maxCap=37 KT with Dry per FCOM Tab 2.29.2a (Good row upper bound also 37 KT).

RWYCC 4 (Medium to Good) advisory limits per Excel 'Medium to Good 788' sheet. Bracket labels shifted down vs Good (max 35, min 10) and slope is gentler (0.0436 vs 0.06). Uniform intercept step +5 yields E9 ≡ 1 KT per %MAC within any bracket. maxCap=null: Excel sheet has no G8 clamp formula — observable output range is [10, 35] KT regardless, so null is the source-faithful and forward-defensive choice.

RWYCC 3 (Medium) advisory limits per Excel 'Medium 788' sheet. First condition backed by variableSlopeBracketedStrategy: each of the 4 brackets carries its own slope (0.032/0.0384/0.0388/0.044). Bracket labels narrowed (max 25, min 10). First condition shipped with decimals=1 — UI must display fractional KT (e.g. 23.9, not 23). maxCap=null: Excel sheet has no G8 clamp.

RWYCC 2 (Medium to Poor) advisory limits per Excel 'Medium to Poor 788' sheet. First condition backed by cgOnlyPiecewiseStrategy — single conditional formula, NO weight dependency. Plateau of 15 KT for CG ≤ 30 %MAC; linear decrease 1 KT per 1.9 %MAC above threshold. Same CG yields same output regardless of weight.

RWYCC 1 (Poor) advisory limit per Excel 'Poor 788' sheet G7 (literal value 10). First condition backed by constantStrategy — output is a single constant regardless of weight, CG, or any other input. Conservative simplification of FCOM RWYCC 1 row (which shows alternating 15/10 pattern) per Q5 user decision: Excel uses the 10 lower-bound uniformly.
